title: Nix & NixOS Setup
summary: Full guide to installing and running Hermes Agent via Nix flake: nix run/profile install for any Nix user, NixOS module for declarative server deployments (native and container modes), secrets management with sops-nix/agenix, MCP server config, plugin installation, and a complete options reference.

env_vars.name: HERMES_HOME
env_vars.purpose: Override the default Hermes data directory; set system-wide when addToSystemPackages = true

env_vars.name: HERMES_MANAGED
env_vars.purpose: Set to true by NixOS systemd service to block imperative CLI config mutations

env_vars.name: HERMES_DEV
env_vars.purpose: Set to 1 to bypass container routing when developing on the hermes codebase

cli_commands.purpose: Run Hermes directly from flake without cloning
Roll back to previous Nix generation
Show the generated config (NixOS managed mode)

config_keys.description: Default model identifier (provider/model format for OpenRouter)

code_examples.caption: Quick start — any Nix user
code_examples.code:
nix run github:NousResearch/hermes-agent -- setup
nix run github:NousResearch/hermes-agent -- chat

code_examples.caption: Minimal NixOS module configuration
sops-nix secrets file
code_examples.code:
# secrets/hermes.yaml (encrypted with sops)
hermes-env: |
    OPENROUTER_API_KEY=sk-or-...
    TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN=123456:ABC...
    ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-...

gotchas: NEVER put API keys in settings or environment — Nix store is world-readable; always use environmentFiles
In managed mode, hermes setup / hermes config set / hermes gateway install are blocked — edit configuration.nix instead
Placing SOUL.md in documents only creates a workspace file, not the main agent persona — SOUL.md must be at $HERMES_HOME/SOUL.md
Changing container.image, extraVolumes, or extraOptions recreates the container and loses the writable layer (apt/pip/npm installs)
Without addToSystemPackages = true, running hermes in a shell creates a separate ~/.hermes/ directory separate from the service state
Podman rootful containers require passwordless sudo — add sudo.extraRules in NixOS config
